_meta.produced_by: Claude (Anthropic) — MK Series AI layer build from DRS papers

paper_title: The Meta-Kaizen Reasoning Network: A Formal Theory of Bisociative Question Structure, Challenge Taxonomy, and Institutional Memory Propagation

phase_ready.note: PH-MK3.1: No empirical data confirms whether club goods mechanism (MK-P2) maintains C1 in practice. PH-MK3.2: Edge-case ambiguity between Challenge Types I and II not yet quantified.

falsification_syntax: I-2 5-part (FALSIFIED IF / WHERE / EVALUATION / BOUNDARY / CONTEXT)
inference_rule_inventory: IR-1 through IR-8 — Fracttalix P2 Build Plan S48

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Derived from minimality proof (Proposition 5.2): constructive counterexample shows each element is necessary.
Grounded in Aristotle's Topics 1.4 (101b11-20) partition of predicables. Stock (1888 §339) exhaustiveness argument.
N=0.86, I'=0.60, C'=0.80, T=1.00. KVS = 0.86 × 0.60 × 0.80 × 1.00 = 0.413. Below kappa=0.50. Explicitly labeled as demonstration of self-referential applicability, not quality evidence.

claim_registry.label: Aristotle's Topics — predicable partition
claim_registry.name: Aristotle's Topics — predicable partition
claim_registry.statement: Aristotle's Topics (1.4, 101b11-20) partitions predicables into four types: definition, genus, property, and accident. Stock (1888 §339) argues this partition is exhaustive for productive questioning about any subject.

claim_registry.label: Koestler's bisociation framework
claim_registry.name: Koestler's bisociation framework
claim_registry.statement: Creative insights arise from simultaneous activation of two habitually incompatible matrices of thought (Koestler 1964). The bisociative bridge between domains produces novel structural analogies.

claim_registry.label: Institutional memory decay
claim_registry.name: Institutional memory decay
claim_registry.statement: Institutional memory decays with personnel transitions. Individual memory is the most volatile retention facility (Walsh & Ungson 1991). Argote & Epple (1990) and Levitt & March (1988) document systematic forgetting curves following personnel transitions.

claim_registry.label: Question Structure Schema (QSS)
claim_registry.name: Question Structure Schema (QSS)
claim_registry.statement: A QSS is a 4-tuple (A, D, P, C) where A = governing Assumption challenged, D = Orthogonal Domain (Jaccard distance > 0.70 from substrate), P = Structural Principle extracted from D, C = Applicability Condition specifying when P applies to the substrate.

claim_registry.label: Challenge Taxonomy (Types I-IV)
claim_registry.name: Challenge Taxonomy (Types I-IV)
claim_registry.statement: Four challenge types derived from Aristotle's predicables: Type I (Scope challenge — assumption applies to wrong domain), Type II (Precision challenge — assumption is vague or ambiguous), Type III (Mechanism challenge — assumption identifies wrong causal mechanism), Type IV (Relevance challenge — assumption is obsolete or irrelevant to current context).

claim_registry.label: Proposition 4.1 — Institutional Memory Loss
claim_registry.name: Proposition 4.1 — Institutional Memory Loss
claim_registry.statement: Under standard improvement record formats (outcome documented, rationale brief or absent, QSS elements not specified), the retention level RL(t) declines with personnel transitions: RL(t_0 + k*Delta) → 0 as k → infinity, where Delta is the median tenure length.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Cited theorem application — Walsh & Ungson (1991): individual memory is most volatile retention facility. QSS elements A and P are predominantly held in individual memory.
inference_rule_steps.output: QSS elements decay with personnel departure.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — each personnel transition eliminates fraction of QSS knowledge. After k transitions: RL ~ (1-d)^k → 0.

WHERE.retention_stable: boolean · true if, in an organization using standard (non-QSS) improvement records, a naive reviewer (no access to original participants) can reconstruct all four QSS elements {A, D, P, C} from records alone after 3+ complete personnel turnovers

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Present improvement records (without QSS) to reviewers with no access to original participants; assess ability to reconstruct A, D, P, C for each record; compute RL as proportion of elements recovered; test after 3+ turnovers; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Partial reconstruction (e.g., P recoverable but A not) → NOT FALSIFIED (RL < 1 confirms decay). Full reconstruction of all 4 elements → FALSIFIED.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Proposition relies on individual memory being the primary storage for rationale. Organizations with strong documentation cultures may have slower decay — but standard formats do not capture QSS elements.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {Organization with standard records, 4 personnel turnovers: naive reviewer reconstructs all 4 QSS elements for 95% of records} → retention_stable = true → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Proposition 5.1 — Challenge Taxonomy Exhaustiveness
claim_registry.name: Proposition 5.1 — Challenge Taxonomy Exhaustiveness
claim_registry.statement: Every challenge to a governing assumption A can be classified into exactly one of four types derived from Aristotle's predicables (Topics 1.4, 101b11-20): Type I (Scope), Type II (Precision), Type III (Mechanism), Type IV (Relevance).

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Cited theorem application — Aristotle's Topics 1.4 partitions predicables exhaustively. Stock (1888 §339) confirms exhaustiveness.
IR-2: Universal Instantiation — map predicable types to challenge types: definition→scope, genus→precision, property→mechanism, accident→relevance.
inference_rule_steps.output: Challenge taxonomy is exhaustive by inheritance from predicable partition.

WHERE.unclassifiable_challenge_exists: boolean · true if there exists a challenge to a governing assumption that cannot be classified into any of Types I-IV, and this unclassifiability is not an edge-case ambiguity between adjacent types

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Present 50 diverse challenges to assumptions across 5 substrate types; classify each into Types I-IV; flag any that resist classification entirely (not merely ambiguous between two types); finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Ambiguity between Types I and II → NOT FALSIFIED (edge-case acknowledged in paper). Challenge that fits none of the four types → FALSIFIED.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Exhaustiveness is grounded in Aristotle's predicable partition, not empirically derived. The mapping from predicables to challenge types is the paper's contribution. Edge-case ambiguity is a classification challenge, not a theoretical failure.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {Challenge that questions the epistemic authority of the assumption-maker, not the assumption content — fits none of Types I-IV} → unclassifiable_challenge_exists = true → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Proposition 5.2 — Minimum Generative Completeness
claim_registry.name: Proposition 5.2 — Minimum Generative Completeness
claim_registry.statement: No proper subset of {A, D, P, C} generates the full space of reusable bisociative insights. Proof by constructive counterexample: removing any single element prevents at least one class of productive reuse.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-4: Definition Expansion — QSS = (A, D, P, C). Consider each 3-element subset.
IR-2: Universal Instantiation — for each subset, construct a reuse scenario that requires the missing element.
inference_rule_steps.output: Without A: cannot reconstruct challenge type. Without D: cannot extend to new substrates. Without P: must re-derive insight from scratch. Without C: indiscriminate application.

WHERE.proper_subset_sufficient: boolean · true if there exists a proper subset of {A, D, P, C} that generates the same space of reusable institutional insights as the full 4-tuple, demonstrated by showing the missing element is always reconstructable from the remaining three

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each 3-element subset: attempt to reconstruct the missing element from the other three across 20 diverse bisociative insights; if reconstruction succeeds in all 20 cases for any subset, proper_subset_sufficient = true; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Occasional reconstruction (e.g., 5/20 cases) → NOT FALSIFIED (partial reconstructability does not imply element is redundant). Systematic reconstruction (20/20) → FALSIFIED.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Minimality proof is by constructive counterexample — one failure per element suffices. The 20-case test is conservative: even one case where reconstruction fails confirms necessity.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {Without D (domain), all 20 insights are fully reusable because P alone specifies the structural analogy} → proper_subset_sufficient = true → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Theorem 5.3 — Library Quality Convergence (conditional)
claim_registry.name: Theorem 5.3 — Library Quality Convergence (conditional)
claim_registry.statement: IF (C1) honest majority of contributing practitioners, AND (C2) retrospective validation each cycle, AND (C3) bounded strategic benefit, THEN Q_L(t) → 1 as t → infinity. This is a conditional design guarantee: C1-C3 jointly characterize the target system, not independent premises.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Modus Ponens — under C1 (honest majority), submitted QSS records are predominantly accurate.
IR-1: Modus Ponens — under C2 (retrospective validation), inaccurate entries are identified and corrected each cycle.
IR-1: Modus Ponens — under C3 (bounded strategic benefit), incentive to submit inaccurate records is bounded, so correction rate exceeds corruption rate.
inference_rule_steps.output: Q_L(t) → 1 as t → infinity by construction.

WHERE.quality_diverges_under_conditions: boolean · true if, in a simulation where C1, C2, and C3 all hold, Q_L(t) fails to converge to 1.0 within 200 cycles

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Simulate library evolution under C1-C3 for 200 cycles: (1) generate QSS entries with C1 honest-majority noise; (2) apply C2 retrospective validation each cycle; (3) bound strategic distortion per C3; (4) compute Q_L(t) as proportion of accurate entries; (5) check Q_L(200) > 0.95; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Q_L(200) > 0.95 → NOT FALSIFIED (convergence on track). Q_L(200) < 0.80 → FALSIFIED (insufficient convergence under all three conditions holding).
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Conditional claim — violation of C1, C2, or C3 does not falsify the theorem. Only falsified if quality fails to converge when all three conditions hold simultaneously.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {All C1-C3 hold, but retrospective validation (C2) has finite detection rate < 1.0, causing Q_L to plateau at 0.75} → quality_diverges_under_conditions = true → FALSIFIED.

placeholder_register.description: No empirical data confirms whether the club goods mechanism (MK-P2) maintains C1 in practice. Theorem 5.3 convergence is conditional.
placeholder_register.target_session: OPEN — resolves with first MKN deployment confirming C1 maintenance

placeholder_register.description: Edge-case ambiguity between Challenge Types I and II not yet quantified. Inter-classifier agreement rate unknown.
placeholder_register.target_session: OPEN — resolves with inter-classifier reliability study

cross_references.relation: QSS generates the Novelty signal via orthogonal domain bisociation.
Library quality convergence parallels governance process-equivalence — both conditional on honest majority.
Institutional memory loss motivates the Virtù Window — organizations lose ability to act when institutional knowledge decays.

self_kvs_demonstration.note: Demonstration of MK-P1 Theorem 4.2 (self-referential applicability). Authors scoring their own paper — threefold circularity explicitly acknowledged. Not quality evidence.
